slides.title: A hot sauna does more than make you *sweat*.
slides.sub: The real payoff doesn't show up in the heat — it shows up that night, in how you relax and how you sleep.

slides.title: Heat melts the tension
slides.body: Heat relaxes tight muscles and triggers *endorphin release*, which may support real stress relief once you cool back down.

slides.title: Heat up before bed
slides.body: A sauna or hot bath *1 to 2 hours before bed* helps your body cool afterward, which can help you fall asleep faster.

slides.title: A pattern worth noting
slides.body: Regular sauna use is *linked to heart health* in research — an association worth knowing, not a cure to count on.

slides.title: Loosen sore muscles
slides.body: Heat *boosts blood flow* to tired, sore muscles, which can support recovery on the days after a hard session.

slides.title: Don't overdo it
slides.body: You lose fluid fast, so *rehydrate and keep it reasonable*. Ask a doctor first if you're pregnant or have heart issues.

slides.title: Sweat now, *sleep better tonight*.
slides.body: Track your sauna sessions and sleep in Vibe Health, and the trend line shows whether heat is actually paying off in your recovery.

caption:
A hot sauna does more than make you sweat — the real payoff shows up that night. 🔥

Heat 1–2 hours before bed for easier sleep, loosen sore muscles, note the heart-health link, and always rehydrate. Save this. 🔖

Track sauna and sleep in Vibe Health to see if the heat is paying off → link in bio.
youtube_title: What a Sauna Really Does for Your Sleep and Recovery
